LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JOHN FRADET'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING LOIS J. BEHNKE OR HER GUARDIAN AD LITEM TO APPEAR AND TESTIFY AT DEPOSITION IS GRANTED IN PART. CCP 1141.24 DOES NOT BAR THIS MOTION OR THE TAKING OF A FURTHER DEPOSITION. IF MS. BEHNKE'S COUNSEL REPRESENTS THAT MS. BEHNKE WILL NOT TESTIFY AT TRIAL AND THE TESTIMONY OF FRANK MCGILL IS BINDING ON MS. BEHNKE AND MR. MCGILL FAMILIARIZES HIMSELF WITH THE DISPUTED ISSUES REGARDING HIS MOTHER'S OWNERSHIP OF THE PROPERTY, MR. FRADET MAY TAKE A FURTHER DEPOSITION OF MR. MCGILL REGARDING THOSE DISPUTED ISSUES. OTHERWISE, MS. BEHNKE OR A GUARDIAN AD LITEM APPOINTED FOR HER MAY BE DEPOSED BY MR. FRADET. THE DEPOSITION MUST OCCUR NO LATER THAN OCTOBER 27, 2017 ON A MUTUALLY CONVENIENT DATE. BOTH SIDES' REQUESTS FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE DENIED SINCE BOTH SIDES HAD SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION FOR THEIR POSITIONS. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: NOT REPORTED. (D302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JOHN FRADET'S MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO COMPLAINT OF DEFENDANT LOIS BEHNKE AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. NOW THAT MR. MCGILL HAS BEEN DEPOSED, THERE IS NO BASIS TO STRIKE MS. BEHNKE'S ANSWER. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED SINCE, BY MAKING MR. MCGILL AVAILABLE FOR A DEPOSITION ON NOVEMBER 7, SOON AFTER HE RETURNED TO THIS COUNTRY, MS. BEHNKE DID NOT ACT WITHOUT SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION REGARDING THE MATTERS RAISED BY THE MOTION. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR NO. 6576, E-MAIL: RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JOHN FRADET'S MOTION TO AMEND COMPLAINT TO ADD FICTITIOUSLY NAMED PARTY AND CAUSE OF ACTION, INCLUDING EXEMPLARY DAMAGES IS GRANTED. THERE IS A LIBERAL POLICY TO ALLOW AMENDMENT AND DEFENDANT CITES NO SALIENT PREJUDICE DUE TO THE ALLEGED DELAY IN FILING THIS MOTION. (SEE FAIR V. BAKHTIARI (2011) 195 CAL.APP.4TH 1135, 1147.) THE COURT NOTES THAT THERE IS NO TRIAL DATE IN THIS ACTION. THE PROPOSED CLAIMS RELATE BACK. "THE RELATION-BACK DOCTRINE REQUIRES THAT THE AMENDED COMPLAINT MUST (1) REST ON THE SAME GENERAL SET OF FACTS, (2) INVOLVE THE SAME INJURY, AND (3) REFER TO THE SAME INSTRUMENTALITY, AS THE ORIGINAL ONE." (NORGART V. UPJOHN CO. (1999) 21 CAL.4TH 383, 408-409 (EMPHASIS IN ORIGINAL.) THE PROPOSED BATTERY CLAIM RELIES ON THE SAME GENERAL SET OF FACTS, INVOLVES THE SAME INJURY, AND REFERS TO THE SAME INSTRUMENTALITY. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE DECLARATION OF PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SUBSTANTIALLY COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1324(B). ROBERT FULTON IS SUBSTITUTED AS DOE 1 IN THE ACTION. THE COURT OVERRULES PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS. MS. TYNAN'S REFERENCE TO THE ARBITRATION AWARD AND THE PARTIES' DISCUSSIONS REGARDING THE AWARD AND RECOVERABLE COSTS ARE NOT BEING OFFERED TO ESTABLISH LIABILITY. (SEE EVID. 1152(A).) WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT.JUDGE: ANGELA BRADSTREET; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; NOT REPORTED. (D302)

MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-13-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF JOHN FRADET'S MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO COMPLAINT of defendant Lois Behnke and for monetary sanctions is DENIED in its entirety. Now that Mr. McGill has been deposed, there is no basis to strike Ms. Behnke's answer. No monetary sanctions are awarded since, by making Mr. McGill available for a deposition on November 7, soon after he returned to this country, Ms. Behnke did not act without substantial justification regarding the matters raised by the motion. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org. (L&M302)

Plaintiff John Fradet Notice Of Motion To Compel Deposition
Off Calendar Sep-25-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Plaintiff John Fradet's Motion For Order Compelling Lois J. Behnke or Her Guardian Ad Litem to Appear and Testify at Deposition is GRANTED in part. CCP 1141.24 does not bar this motion or the taking of a further deposition. If Ms. Behnke's counsel represents that Ms. Behnke will not testify at trial and the testimony of Frank McGill is binding on Ms. Behnke and Mr. McGill familiarizes himself with the disputed issues regarding his mother's ownership of the property, Mr. Fradet may take a further deposition of Mr. McGill regarding those disputed issues. Otherwise, Ms. Behnke or a guardian ad litem appointed for her may be deposed by Mr. Fradet. The deposition must occur no later than October 27, 2017 on a mutually convenient date. Both sides' requests for monetary sanctions are denied since both sides had substantial justification for their positions. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Not reported. (D302)

Notice Of Motion To Amend Complaint To Add Fictitiously Named Party And Cause Of Action, Including Exemplary Damages
Off Calendar Aug-02-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF JOHN FRADET's Motion To Amend Complaint To Add Fictitiously Named Party And Cause Of Action, Including Exemplary Damages is granted. There is a liberal policy to allow amendment and defendant cites no salient prejudice due to the alleged delay in filing this motion. (See Fair v. Bakhtiari (2011) 195 Cal.App.4th 1135, 1147.) The court notes that there is no trial date in this action. The proposed claims relate back. "The relation-back doctrine requires that the amended complaint must (1) rest on the same general set of facts, (2) involve the same injury, and (3) refer to the same instrumentality, as the original one." (Norgart v. Upjohn Co. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 383, 408-409 (emphasis in original.) The proposed battery claim relies on the same general set of facts, involves the same injury, and refers to the same instrumentality. The court finds that the declaration of plaintiff's counsel substantially complies with CRC 3.1324(b). Robert Fulton is substituted as Doe 1 in the action. The court overrules plaintiff's evidentiary objections. Ms. Tynan's reference to the arbitration award and the parties' discussions regarding the award and recoverable costs are not being offered to establish liability. (See Evid. 1152(a).) Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Angela Bradstreet; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)
